questions.question_text: On the whole, how satisfied are you with the way democracy works in Canada?
How much would you say the political system in Canada allows people like you to have an influence on politics?
I will accept the outcome of the next Canadian election, regardless of who wins.
Individuals in Canada are free to express their personal views on political topics without fear of surveillance or punishment.
Individuals in Canada are free to practice and express their religious faith or nonbelief in public.
Individuals can freely assemble in Canada (e.g., freedom to protest peacefully, engage in petitions, organize public events).
The Canadian justice system treats everyone equally.
Having a strong leader who does not have to bother with parliament and elections.
Having experts, not government, make decisions according to what they think is best for the country.
Having a representative democracy where citizens vote to elect their representatives that make decisions on their behalf.
The people in Canada are divided.
There are divisions between Canadians that cannot be overcome.
On which issue do you think people in Canada are most divided?

response_options.label: COVID-19, vaccines and public health policies
Politics and the role of government
The economy, class, or socioeconomic status
